If bearing is +107 degrees and yaw is 0, the goal is 107 degrees to the right of the current heading. This would be "far_right" or "behind_right" depending on the convention.

Actually, looking at the experience more carefully:
- [experience] When behind_left & far: TURN_RIGHT → neutral (avg +0cm, seen 14x). [behind_left, far] TURN_RIGHT → no change. Turned away from goal (bearing was behind_left). Turn the other way.

This suggests that when the goal is behind_left, turning right moves away from it. So the bearing convention seems to be:
- Positive bearing = right side
- Negative bearing = left side
